h1: IB Tutors in Greater Kailash, Delhi
heroTitle: IB Tutoring for Greater Kailash Families
heroSubtitle: Greater Kailash sits at the heart of South Delhi's international-school corridor, and the academic pressure here is real. IB Gram helps families find tutors for DP, MYP and PYP across Math, Sciences, Economics and English. Availability depends on subject, schedule, exact location within the area, tutor fit and preferred mode — home, online or hybrid — so early planning makes a meaningful difference.
introSummary: Greater Kailash is one of South Delhi's most established residential neighbourhoods, close to several international and IB-affiliated schools. Families here often need focused, flexible support for demanding DP subjects and internal assessments, and IB Gram is built precisely for that kind of careful, qualified match.

contentBlocks.heading: Why Greater Kailash Families Look for IB Tutoring
contentBlocks.body:
Greater Kailash — split across GK I and GK II — has long been one of South Delhi's preferred addresses for professional families, many of whom have children enrolled in international or IB-framework schools nearby. The locality sits within easy reach of schools in Saket, Defence Colony and Vasant Vihar, which means the concentration of IB students here is noticeably high relative to much of the rest of the city.

That proximity to international schools is a double-edged reality. The academic culture in these households tends to be ambitious, but the IB Diploma Programme in particular demands a kind of structured independent thinking that standard coaching cannot provide. Parents in Greater Kailash frequently describe the same concern: their child works hard, attends school conscientiously, but still struggles to connect the dots between command terms, mark schemes and their own written responses. The gap is rarely about effort — it is usually about method.

IB Gram was designed to address exactly that gap: matching students with tutors who understand the IB's internal logic, not just the content, and who can work around the tight evening schedules that Greater Kailash students typically carry.
items: High density of IB students across GK I and GK II households
DP subjects requiring method-focused, not content-only support
Evening and weekend scheduling pressure common to South Delhi families
Proximity to Saket, Defence Colony and Vasant Vihar school corridors

contentBlocks.heading: IB Programmes Covered — PYP, MYP and the Diploma
contentBlocks.body:
IB Gram supports students across all three main IB programmes, and the needs at each level are genuinely different.

For PYP families in Greater Kailash, the focus is usually on nurturing inquiry-based habits early — helping younger children understand why they are being asked to think a certain way, not just what to produce. MYP support often involves criterion-based assessment, personal project preparation and building the kind of self-directed study skills that will become non-negotiable in the DP years.

The Diploma Programme is where most tutoring requests in this area originate. HL subjects — particularly Math Analysis and Approaches, Physics and Chemistry — demand a level of precision and conceptual depth that many students find difficult to develop without individual attention. Subject-specific help with Internal Assessments (the IA) is frequently requested: students need a thinking partner who can help them sharpen a research question, understand the assessment criteria and structure their analysis clearly — while keeping all the actual work genuinely their own.

Extended Essay support follows similar lines: choosing a focused topic, understanding the academic register expected at EE level and hitting school deadlines without sacrificing depth. TOK is another area where a tutor who truly understands the course can make sessions feel purposeful rather than abstract.
items: DP HL and SL subject tutoring with attention to command terms and mark schemes
Internal Assessment guidance — sharpening research questions and analytical structure
Extended Essay topic selection, drafting and school-timeline management
TOK essay and exhibition preparation with conceptual clarity

contentBlocks.heading: Subjects Most Requested by Greater Kailash Students
contentBlocks.body:
The subject requests IB Gram sees from South Delhi families cluster fairly predictably around the DP's most demanding Higher Level options. Math Analysis and Approaches HL is consistently the highest-priority request — it is the subject that most directly affects a student's university choices, and the gap between what school lessons cover and what HL Paper 3 actually requires can be significant. Math Applications and Interpretation HL is increasingly common too, particularly among students balancing sciences and economics simultaneously.

Physics HL and Chemistry HL follow closely. Both involve extended practical work, data analysis in the IA, and a style of examination questioning that rewards methodical problem-solving over pattern recognition. Biology is requested with somewhat less frequency from this area but remains a consistent need.

Economics is perhaps the subject where Greater Kailash students most benefit from a tutor who understands the specific IB assessment style — the internal assessment commentaries, the command-term hierarchy and the way real-world examples need to be integrated rather than simply cited. English A: Language and Literature and English A: Literature round out the common requests, often in the context of oral preparation and essay structure.

Language B tutoring — French, Spanish, Hindi — is also available through IB Gram's broader network for students who need support beyond the core sciences and mathematics.
items: Math AA HL and AI HL — problem-solving depth and examination technique
Physics and Chemistry HL — IA data analysis and conceptual reasoning
Economics HL — commentary structure and real-world integration
English A — oral preparation, essay structure and literary analysis

contentBlocks.heading: Home Tutoring in Greater Kailash — Practicalities and Nearby Coverage
contentBlocks.body:
Greater Kailash's internal road network is well established, and in practical terms most parts of GK I and GK II are accessible without the severe congestion that affects parts of Central and East Delhi. That said, South Delhi traffic between roughly 5 pm and 8 pm can add meaningful time to any tutor's commute, which is why session start times in the 4:30–5:00 pm or 7:00 pm-plus windows tend to work more reliably for home visits.

For tutors travelling from other parts of South Delhi — Defence Colony, Hauz Khas, Green Park, Panchsheel Park or New Friends Colony — the distances are modest and travel windows are workable on most weekdays. Families in nearby Jor Bagh or Saket can also access IB Gram tutors without difficulty, and IB Gram's matching process takes exact travel practicality into account rather than relying solely on postcode proximity.

Where a suitable tutor is not immediately available for in-home sessions, or where a student's schedule is genuinely fragmented across school activities and weekend commitments, online tutoring is a practical and often equally effective alternative. Many Greater Kailash families use a hybrid model — weekly in-person sessions for subjects requiring worked examples on paper, and online check-ins for essay feedback and IA review. City-level fallback through IB Gram's Delhi-wide network ensures that subject availability gaps at the hyper-local level rarely translate into an actual block for a student.
items: GK I and GK II home sessions workable from Defence Colony, Hauz Khas and Panchsheel Park tutors
Evening traffic patterns favour 4:30 pm or post-7 pm home session start times
Hybrid model common — in-person for problem sets, online for essay and IA feedback
Delhi-wide tutor network as fallback when local availability is limited

contentBlocks.heading: How IB Gram Matches a Tutor for Your Child
contentBlocks.body:
The matching process at IB Gram starts with the specifics, not the generalities. A parent filling out a request for a Greater Kailash student is asked about the programme and year group, the specific subjects and levels (HL or SL), the school's internal calendar and upcoming assessment deadlines, the student's preferred working style, and whether home, online or hybrid sessions are the priority.

That information is then used to identify tutors whose subject knowledge, IB programme experience and practical availability align with what the student actually needs — not simply whoever is geographically closest. A tutor who has worked extensively with DP Chemistry IA students and understands how particular schools structure their internal deadlines will almost always be a better fit than a general science graduate available in the right postcode.

For Greater Kailash families, the matching process also considers the rhythm of school life here: competitive extracurriculars, late-evening classes, mock examination seasons in October-November and February-March, and the specific crunch that precedes IA submission windows. The goal is a tutor who can adapt to that rhythm rather than add another scheduling pressure to it.
items: Matching on programme, subject, level and school internal calendar — not only location
Tutor IB programme experience weighted alongside subject knowledge
Schedule compatibility assessed for South Delhi school rhythms and activity loads
Mode preference — home, online or hybrid — incorporated from the first request

contentBlocks.heading: Home, Online and Hybrid — What Works in Greater Kailash
contentBlocks.body:
Home tutoring in Greater Kailash works well when the subject demands a shared physical workspace — mathematical problem-solving, worked chemistry calculations, physics diagrams and the kind of back-and-forth that benefits from paper and a whiteboard. Families with a dedicated study room and predictable after-school timings tend to find in-home sessions the most productive. The consistency of a tutor arriving at the same time each week also builds a working rhythm that many IB students find grounding during an inherently pressured academic year.

Online tutoring has become a genuinely preferred choice for many South Delhi families, not simply a fallback. For subjects like Economics, English A and TOK — where much of the session involves reading, discussing and refining written work — a shared screen and document environment can be as effective as a physical meeting, sometimes more so because it removes commute time from both sides. Students preparing for IA and EE often find that online review sessions are actually easier to schedule around school activities.

Hybrid works particularly well for students juggling multiple HL subjects. They may attend in-person sessions for Math AA or Physics once or twice a week, and use online slots for Economics commentary review or English essay feedback on a more flexible basis. IB Gram can support any combination of these modes.
items: Home sessions best for problem-solving subjects requiring written working
Online preferred for essay subjects, IA review and flexible scheduling
Hybrid common among students with multiple HL subjects and activity commitments
Mode can shift across the academic year as deadlines and schedules evolve

contentBlocks.heading: How Tutors on IB Gram Are Reviewed
contentBlocks.body:
IB Gram applies a subject-and-programme review process before tutors are matched with students. This involves checking that a tutor's claimed IB experience is credible — whether through their own academic background, prior tutoring experience with IB students or demonstrated familiarity with the specific assessment criteria and command terms relevant to the subjects they cover.

Tutors are not simply listed on the basis of a qualification. The review process looks at IB-specific competency: does this person understand the difference between a level-5 and level-7 IA? Can they explain the DP assessment model clearly to a parent? Do they know how the EE is graded and what school deadlines typically look like in a Delhi international school context?

Parent communication is a practical part of how IB Gram operates. After the initial match, parents can flag if the tutor's approach, communication style or scheduling reliability is not working — and IB Gram will revisit the match. The first session is typically treated as a trial, giving both sides the chance to assess fit before a longer-term commitment is made. No tutoring relationship should feel like a fixed arrangement from day one.
items: IB programme knowledge verified — not only academic qualifications
Assessment criteria and command term familiarity checked per subject
Parent feedback loop built into the post-match process
First session treated as a trial for mutual fit assessment

contentBlocks.heading: The School Ecosystem Around Greater Kailash
contentBlocks.body:
Greater Kailash sits within the part of South Delhi that has some of the highest concentrations of international and IB-framework schools in the city. The British School New Delhi, located in Chanakyapuri, and the American Embassy School are among the internationally recognised institutions whose students live across South Delhi's premium residential areas, including Greater Kailash. DPS International Saket, closer in proximity to GK I and GK II, is another school whose students regularly seek IB tutoring support.

Beyond these, there are several other schools in the broader Saket, Defence Colony, Vasant Vihar and Hauz Khas belt whose students find their way into IB Gram's matching process. The school context matters because each institution has its own internal assessment calendar, mock examination schedule and IA submission timeline — and a tutor who is familiar with how these typically unfold in Delhi's international school environment can be meaningfully more useful than one who is not.

IB Gram is an independent tutoring platform. The mention of any school here is for geographic and contextual reference only, and IB Gram has no official affiliation with any school or institution unless specifically stated.
items: South Delhi school corridor includes British School New Delhi, AES and DPS International Saket
Each school carries its own internal assessment and examination calendar
Tutor familiarity with Delhi international school timelines is part of the matching process
Greater Kailash students often attend schools in Saket, Vasant Vihar and Chanakyapuri

contentBlocks.heading: What IB Gram Can and Cannot Promise
contentBlocks.body:
Honest expectations matter more than reassuring claims, and IB Gram takes that seriously. Availability of tutors for any given subject, level, schedule and location is genuine — it depends on who is currently active in the network and what their commitments allow. While IB Gram works to match requests promptly, early planning — ideally before the academic year begins or well ahead of a key IA deadline — almost always leads to a better outcome than a last-minute search during a mock examination window.

IB Gram does not guarantee grades, predicted scores, university admission outcomes or specific examination results. A tutor's role is to help a student develop understanding, improve their approach to assessments and build the independent thinking that the IB genuinely rewards. The quality and consistency of that support matters — but so does the student's own effort, school attendance and engagement with the programme.

On academic honesty: IB tutors on this platform are expected to guide thinking, help structure arguments and review work against assessment criteria. They do not write Internal Assessments, Extended Essays or TOK essays on a student's behalf. The IB's academic integrity policies exist to protect the meaning of the qualification, and every tutor matched through IB Gram is expected to work within those boundaries.
items: Availability varies by subject, level, schedule and mode — early requests help
No guarantees of grades, predicted scores or admission outcomes
Tutors guide thinking and structure; assessed work remains the student's own
Academic honesty boundaries respected across all IA, EE and TOK support

contentBlocks.heading: Starting the Search for an IB Tutor in Greater Kailash
contentBlocks.body:
If you are a parent in Greater Kailash — or nearby in Defence Colony, Hauz Khas, New Friends Colony or Panchsheel Park — and your child is navigating the IB Diploma, MYP or PYP, IB Gram is a practical starting point. The process begins with a free academic consultation where you share the specifics: subject, level, school, upcoming deadlines and preferred tutoring mode. From there, IB Gram works to identify tutors whose experience and availability are genuinely aligned with what your child needs.

There are no guarantees of a perfect match in every case or in every timeline — but the matching process is designed to be honest about fit rather than simply filling a slot. If the first tutor does not feel right, the conversation continues. The goal is a working relationship that holds up through the full pressure of an IB academic year, not just the first few sessions.

Book a free consultation through the IB Gram website to get the process started. The earlier you begin, the more options are likely to be available.
items: Free academic consultation — no obligation to commit upfront
Matching based on subject, level, school timeline and preferred mode
First-session trial before any longer-term arrangement is confirmed
Greater Kailash and all nearby South Delhi areas covered

faqs.question: Are there IB tutors available for home sessions in Greater Kailash specifically, or only online?
faqs.answer: IB Gram supports home, online and hybrid tutoring for families in Greater Kailash. Home sessions are available depending on which tutors are currently active in the area, their own commute range and your scheduling preferences. Availability varies by subject and timing — the 4:30–5:00 pm and post-7 pm windows tend to work most reliably for in-home visits in South Delhi. If an in-home match is not immediately available for your specific subject, IB Gram can offer online or hybrid options while the search continues.

faqs.question: My child is in DP Year 1 and struggling with Math AA HL. How quickly can IB Gram find a tutor?
faqs.answer: Math AA HL is one of the most requested subjects through IB Gram in South Delhi, and there is usually tutor availability — but timing matters. Matching a student with the right tutor for HL Mathematics takes into account their current school syllabus position, upcoming assessments and preferred working style, which means the process is more careful than a simple directory lookup. Starting early in the academic year, before mock examination seasons and IA deadlines create scheduling pressure, tends to lead to the most settled and productive matches.

faqs.question: How does IB Gram handle Internal Assessment support without crossing academic honesty lines?
faqs.answer: Tutors matched through IB Gram are expected to help students understand the assessment criteria, refine their research question, strengthen the logical structure of their analysis and check their work against the relevant rubric. What tutors do not do is write sections of the IA, choose the topic on the student's behalf or produce analysis that the student then submits as their own. The IB's academic integrity policies are clear on this, and every tutor in the IB Gram network is expected to work within them. The student's voice, thinking and conclusions must be genuinely their own.

faqs.question: Can a single tutor cover both Physics HL and Chemistry HL for my child?
faqs.answer: It is possible, and some tutors in IB Gram's network do have strong competency across both HL sciences. However, the matching process does not assume this by default — subject depth and familiarity with IB-specific assessment criteria are checked separately for each subject. If your child needs support in both Physics and Chemistry HL, IB Gram will look for a tutor who can genuinely cover both at the required level, and if that is not the right fit, two separate tutors may be recommended. The goal is quality of support, not convenience of consolidation.

faqs.question: My child attends a school in Saket. Can a Greater Kailash tutor also help with school-specific deadlines and mock exam timing?
faqs.answer: Yes — familiarity with how Delhi international schools typically structure their internal calendars is part of what IB Gram considers in the matching process. Schools in the Saket and South Delhi belt generally follow predictable IA submission windows and mock examination cycles, and tutors who have worked with students from these schools will be aware of those rhythms. That said, IB Gram is not officially affiliated with any school, so specific internal deadlines should always be confirmed directly with the school.

faqs.question: Is IB Gram officially affiliated with The British School New Delhi, American Embassy School or DPS International Saket?
faqs.answer: No. IB Gram is an independent tutoring platform and is not officially affiliated with these schools unless specifically stated. References to schools on this page are for geographic and contextual relevance only — to help families in Greater Kailash understand the broader school environment in South Delhi. Any tutoring support arranged through IB Gram is independent of the schools themselves, and parents should always engage directly with their child's school for official academic guidance.

finalCta: Book a free academic consultation with IB Gram to find a qualified IB tutor in Greater Kailash or nearby South Delhi. Share your child's subject, level and schedule, and IB Gram will work to match you with a tutor whose experience and availability are genuinely aligned with what you need — no guarantees, no pressure, just a careful, honest process.
